items.title: DRC: Ebola outbreak 2026
items.summary: On 15 May 2026, authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) declared an Ebola outbreak after cases were confirmed in Ituri and North Kivu provinces, with further cases later confirmed in South Kivu.
items.content: DRC: Ebola outbreak 2026. On 15 May 2026, authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) declared an Ebola outbreak after cases were confirmed in Ituri and North Kivu provinces, with further cases later confirmed in South Kivu.. Health

items.title: Sudan: risk analysis (2026)
items.summary: This report outlines seven risks of emerging or deteriorating humanitarian conditions in Sudan between June–December 2026. The risk analysis aims to support humanitarian decision makers in anticipating potential escalations in humanitarian needs, displacement, humanitarian access constraints, and service disruptions to inform proactive preparedness and response planning.
items.content: Sudan: risk analysis (2026). This report outlines seven risks of emerging or deteriorating humanitarian conditions in Sudan between June–December 2026. The risk analysis aims to support humanitarian decision makers in anticipating potential escalations in humanitarian needs, displacement, humanitarian access constraints, and service disruptions to inform proactive preparedness and response planning.

items.title: Sudan: the six grave violations against children during armed conflict
items.summary: This report aims to analyse the situation of children affected by armed conflict in Sudan through the UNSC’s framework of the six grave violations against children. It focuses on developments in 2025 and 2026, drawing on 2024 data where more recent information is unavailable.
items.content: Sudan: the six grave violations against children during armed conflict. This report aims to analyse the situation of children affected by armed conflict in Sudan through the UNSC’s framework of the six grave violations against children. It focuses on developments in 2025 and 2026, drawing on 2024 data where more recent information is unavailable.. Protection

items.title: Mali: escalation of conflict in Northern and Central regions
items.summary: Coordinated attacks on 25–26 April have caused acute protection and access concerns for civilians in several urban centres and along key transport corridors in Mali, particularly in Bamako/Kati, Bourem, Gao, Kidal, Mopti, and Sévaré.
items.content: Mali: escalation of conflict in Northern and Central regions. Coordinated attacks on 25–26 April have caused acute protection and access concerns for civilians in several urban centres and along key transport corridors in Mali, particularly in Bamako/Kati, Bourem, Gao, Kidal, Mopti, and Sévaré.. Conflict and violence

items.title: Sudan: Analysis of humanitarian access in the Darfur and Kordofan regions
items.summary: This report aims to inform the humanitarian community and decision makers interested in the Sudanese context of recent humanitarian access developments, emerging trends, and anticipatory access risks in the Darfur and Kordofan regions. It is an update to this April 2025 report
items.content: Sudan: Analysis of humanitarian access in the Darfur and Kordofan regions. This report aims to inform the humanitarian community and decision makers interested in the Sudanese context of recent humanitarian access developments, emerging trends, and anticipatory access risks in the Darfur and Kordofan regions. It is an update to this April 2025 report. Humanitarian access

items.title: Niger: violence against civilians in western regions
items.summary: This report analyses the humanitarian consequences of a marked escalation in violence against civilians in Niger’s western regions in 2025, reflected in both an increase in the number of incidents and a sharp rise in fatalities in events coded as violence against civilians.
items.content: Niger: violence against civilians in western regions. This report analyses the humanitarian consequences of a marked escalation in violence against civilians in Niger’s western regions in 2025, reflected in both an increase in the number of incidents and a sharp rise in fatalities in events coded as violence against civilians.. Conflict and violence

items.title: South Sudan: Conflict and displacement in Jonglei
items.summary: By 13 February 2026, renewed conflict in Jonglei state had displaced at least 280,000 people, with population movements accelerating as front lines shifted across northern counties.
items.content: South Sudan: Conflict and displacement in Jonglei. By 13 February 2026, renewed conflict in Jonglei state had displaced at least 280,000 people, with population movements accelerating as front lines shifted across northern counties.. Conflict and violence

items.title: Madagascar: Pre-crisis profile - Tropical Cyclone Gezani
items.summary: Tropical Cyclone Gezani made landfall in Madagascar on 11 February 2026 as a Category 3 tropical cyclone, hitting the Ankazobe district of the Analamanga region. The cyclone has generated sustained winds of up to 180km/h, gusts estimated at up to 250km/h, intense rainfall, and coastal flooding.
items.content: Madagascar: Pre-crisis profile - Tropical Cyclone Gezani. Tropical Cyclone Gezani made landfall in Madagascar on 11 February 2026 as a Category 3 tropical cyclone, hitting the Ankazobe district of the Analamanga region. The cyclone has generated sustained winds of up to 180km/h, gusts estimated at up to 250km/h, intense rainfall, and coastal flooding.. Natural hazards

items.title: Sudan: Conflict-induced displacement in North and South Kordofan
items.summary: Since 25 October 2025, conflict between the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) in the Greater Kordofan region of Sudan has sparked widespread displacement. After RSF’s October 2025 takeover of Al Fasher, North Darfur, from SAF, the Kordofan states have emerged as the new front lines as RSF and allied groups look to expand their areas of control.
items.content: Sudan: Conflict-induced displacement in North and South Kordofan. Since 25 October 2025, conflict between the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) and Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) in the Greater Kordofan region of Sudan has sparked widespread displacement. Conflict and violence

items.title: Mozambique: impact of flooding in Gaza, Maputo, Niassa, Sofala, and Zambezia provinces
items.summary: This report provides an overview of flooding and its humanitarian impacts in Mozambique. The report aims to inform the humanitarian community of both the immediate and mediumterm impacts of flooding in Mozambique’s most affected provinces and support response operations.
items.content: Mozambique: impact of flooding in Gaza, Maputo, Niassa, Sofala, and Zambezia provinces. This report provides an overview of flooding and its humanitarian impacts in Mozambique. The report aims to inform the humanitarian community of both the immediate and mediumterm impacts of flooding in Mozambique’s most affected provinces and support response operations.

items.title: Sudan: digital harms in conflict zones
items.summary: This report provides an overview of the types of digital threats and harms reported publicly in Sudan, highlighting the impacts on civilians and humanitarian responders. The report stresses the critical importance of understanding these harms – including weaponised disinformation, connectivity blackouts, and surveillance – because of their very real and profound offline consequences and offers concrete, illustrative examples from the Sudanese context.
items.content: Sudan: digital harms in conflict zones. The report stresses the critical importance of understanding these harms – including weaponised disinformation, connectivity blackouts, and surveillance – because of their very real and profound offline consequences and offers concrete, illustrative examples from the Sudanese context.. Conflict and violence

items.title: Kenya: Anticipated impact of drought conditions
items.summary: This report examines projected drought conditions in a select sample of northern and eastern counties (Mandera, Tana River, Turkana, and Wajir) in ASAL regions that climate forecasts and early warning indicators project will experience some of the most severe impacts.
items.content: Kenya: Anticipated impact of drought conditions. This report examines projected drought conditions in a select sample of northern and eastern counties (Mandera, Tana River, Turkana, and Wajir) in ASAL regions that climate forecasts and early warning indicators project will experience some of the most severe impacts.. Natural hazards

items.title: Sudan: economic impacts and emerging trends in West and Central Darfur
items.summary: This analysis focuses on West and Central Darfur as the dynamics of economic collapse are more acute and severe in these states than in other parts of the region. The report fills a critical gap by providing a targeted and in-depth examination of the specific economic impacts in these areas, including the neartotal breakdown of formal intra-Darfur and cross-border commercial systems.
items.content: Sudan: economic impacts and emerging trends in West and Central Darfur. This analysis focuses on West and Central Darfur as the dynamics of economic collapse are more acute and severe in these states than in other parts of the region. The report fills a critical gap by providing a targeted and in-depth examination of the specific economic impacts in these areas, including the neartotal breakdown of formal intra-Darfur and cross-border commercial systems.. Economy

items.title: Sudan: scenarios (2026)
items.summary: This report outlinines four scenarios that explore the ways governance and control in Sudan could develop until December 2026 and the potential impacts on people and the humanitarian response. The scenarios consider impacts on the economy, services, access, and humanitarian conditions.
items.content: Sudan: scenarios (2026). This report outlinines four scenarios that explore the ways governance and control in Sudan could develop until December 2026 and the potential impacts on people and the humanitarian response. The scenarios consider impacts on the economy, services, access, and humanitarian conditions.. Anticipatory analysis

items.title: Sudan: escalation of violence in Al Fasher
items.summary: Following their takeover of Al Fasher on 26 October 2025, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) have committed widespread mass atrocities against civilians. Reports include mass killings, summary executions, mass detentions, kidnappings, and sexual violence, particularly targeting women and girls.
items.content: Sudan: escalation of violence in Al Fasher. Following their takeover of Al Fasher on 26 October 2025, the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) have committed widespread mass atrocities against civilians. Reports include mass killings, summary executions, mass detentions, kidnappings, and sexual violence, particularly targeting women and girls.. Conflict and violence

items.title: Nigeria: conflict dynamics and humanitarian implications in North East, North West, and North Central
items.summary: This report provides an analytical overview of the escalating, complex, and interlinked violence occurring across Nigeria’s North East, North West, and North Central geopolitical zones.
items.content: Nigeria: conflict dynamics and humanitarian implications in North East, North West, and North Central. This report provides an analytical overview of the escalating, complex, and interlinked violence occurring across Nigeria’s North East, North West, and North Central geopolitical zones.. items.title: Somalia: anticipated effects of drought on malnutrition drivers
items.summary: The report aims to provide an overview and analysis of the current and anticipated impacts of drought in Somalia on the drivers that cause malnutrition, such as food insecurity, displacement, disease outbreaks, and conflict.
items.content: Somalia: anticipated effects of drought on malnutrition drivers. Natural hazards

items.title: Sudan: Food security and nutrition analysis - barriers, impacts, and opportunities
items.summary: This report examines the challenges shaping food security and nutrition analysis in Sudan and how these constraints affect the production of reliable and consolidated evidence. The report considers the impact of these barriers on humanitarian response and identifies opportunities to strengthen collective analysis.
items.content: Sudan: Food security and nutrition analysis - barriers, impacts, and opportunities. The report considers the impact of these barriers on humanitarian response and identifies opportunities to strengthen collective analysis.. Food security & livelihoods, Nutrition
